manufacturers.name: DIY (open-source, "like Beacon RevH but open source")
Proto-pasta
manufacturers.specialty: Niche functional/composite PLA specialist (conductive, magnetic, metal-filled).

manufacturers.name: MatterHackers
manufacturers.specialty: Large US retailer + house brands spanning budget→engineering; broad selection.

manufacturers.name: Inland
manufacturers.specialty: Budget value brand sold via Micro Center/Amazon; rebadged reliable PLA/PETG.

specs.Current (A): ~1.2 A RMS (≈2 A peak); code cap 2.0 A
specs.Notestypicaluse: Older SPI driver. Sensorless homing via SG2. Supports SPI daisy-chain.
specs.Notes: Older SPI driver; sensorless via SG2 (sgt), CoolStep, SPI daisy-chain.

specs.Current (A): ~1.2 A RMS (≈2 A peak); code cap 2.0 A
specs.Notestypicaluse: Silent budget driver. No sensorless homing, no CoolStep. Drop-in for X/Y/Z/E on quiet builds.
specs.Sensemosfet: Internal MOSFETs; external sense resistors; Rds-on ≈ 0.28 Ω HS+LS (typ) (approx)
specs.Notes: Silent budget driver. No StallGuard ⇒ no sensorless homing, no CoolStep. Datasheet shared with TMC2202/TMC2224.

specs.Current (A): ~1.4–1.7 A RMS (≈2.8 A peak); code cap 2.0 A
specs.Notestypicaluse: The default modern UART driver. Supports sensorless homing on X/Y. Very common on Voron/Ender boards.
specs.Sensemosfet: Internal MOSFETs; external sense; Rds-on 170 mΩ HS & 170 mΩ LS (typ @25 °C)
specs.Notes: The modern default UART driver; sensorless homing on X/Y, CoolStep. Very common on Voron/Ender boards.

specs.Current (A): ~1.2 A RMS (≈2 A peak); code cap 2.0 A
specs.Currentphase: 1.4 A RMS cont.; 1.77 A RMS max (0.11 Ω sense)
specs.Notestypicaluse: Pin/register-compatible refresh of the 2208 (QFN package, better thermals). In Klipper configure as [tmc2208 …].
specs.Sensemosfet: Internal MOSFETs; external sense; Rds-on ≈ 0.28 Ω HS+LS (typ) (approx)
specs.Notes: Drop-in refresh of the TMC2208 in a QFN package (better thermals). In Klipper configured as [tmc2208 …].

specs.Current (A): ~1.4–2.0 A RMS (≈2.8 A peak); code cap 2.0 A
specs.Notestypicaluse: Improved/robust 2209 (better thermal pad, ESD). In Klipper configure as [tmc2209 …]; same sensorless support.
specs.Sensemosfet: Internal MOSFETs; external sense; low Rds-on (improved thermal pad vs 2209)
specs.Notes: Robust/improved TMC2209 (ESD, thermal). In Klipper configured as [tmc2209 …]; same sensorless support.

specs.Current (A): ~2.0–3.0 A RMS (board/Rref/current_range dep.); current set via GLOBALSCALER+current_range
specs.Notestypicaluse: Modern high-current driver with on-chip ADC temp/supply sense + over-temp/over-voltage prewarning. Common on newer boards (e.g. as a 2209 upgrade). Runs hot at high PWM freq.
specs.Sensemosfet: Two integrated 36 V H-bridges with non-dissipative Integrated Current Sensing (ICS) — no external sense resistor; full-scale set via external Rref + current-range bit
specs.Notes: Smart high-current driver with on-chip ADC temp/supply sense + over-temp/over-voltage prewarning. Runs hot at high PWM freq.

specs.Currentphase: ~2.8 A RMS (datasheet); up to ~4 A per output with heatsinking
specs.Notestypicaluse: Older mid-power SPI driver (e.g. original Duet/RADDS-era boards). No StealthChop, so not silent. get_tmc_frequency() returns None.
specs.Notes: Older mid-power SPI driver (original Duet/RADDS-era). No StealthChop ⇒ not silent. CoolStep (SMARTEN).

specs.Peak (A): up to 20 A coil with external N-MOSFETs (datasheet max)
specs.Currentphase: Board/FET-limited; commonly ~3 A RMS internal, higher with external FETs
specs.Sensemosfet: Drives external N-channel MOSFETs (gate driver on-chip); external sense resistors
specs.Notes: High-power / high-voltage part for big motors (large CoreXY, IDEX, gantries). Has an on-chip motion controller but Klipper drives it step/dir.

specs.Current (A): ~3 A RMS internal FETs; board-limited, often 4.5 A+ with externals (code cap 10.6 A)
specs.Notestypicaluse: High-power / high-voltage driver for big motors (large CoreXY, IDEX, gantries). tmc5160.py header covers TMC5160/TMC2160; the 5161 is the integrated-FET sibling and uses the same module. Has an on-chip motion controller, but Klipper drives it step/dir like the others.

specs.Sensemosfet: Integrated power MOSFETs (integrated-FET sibling of the external-FET TMC5160)
specs.Notes: Integrated-FET version of the TMC5160 family; same SPI register model and Klipper module (tmc5160).

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: Dual full-bridge DMOS; external sense; synchronous rectification
specs.Notes: Dual full-bridge PWM driver (no built-in microstep indexer); used as 2-phase stepper stage (e.g. some toolboards).
Translator-based DMOS driver w/ automatic current decay; A4988 relative.

specs.RMS (A): 2 A peak / ~1.2 A cont (carrier, w/ cooling)
specs.Notes: The classic RepRap driver; translator + overcurrent protection.

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: DMOS; external sense; QuietStep adaptive decay
specs.Notes: Quieter A4988-class part via QuietStep adaptive current control.

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: Dual full-bridge DMOS; external sense; synchronous rectification
specs.Notes: Dual full-bridge PWM driver w/ independent channel control; DC-motor part also usable as a stepper power stage.

specs.Package: Heatsink/airflow needed near rated current; ~1 A/phase safe bare
specs.Notes: DMOS microstepping driver + translator, mixed-decay, overcurrent protection. The classic RepRap driver.

specs.Package: QuietStep adaptive decay lowers noise; heatsink near rating
specs.Notes: DMOS microstepping driver + translator, QuietStep adaptive current control, 1/32 µstep, up to 40 V/±2 A.

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: Dual A4950 full-bridge drivers; STM32F103CB (M3, 72 MHz); TLE5012B magnetic encoder; OLED
specs.Notes: Closed-loop step compensation; runs stock firmware or SimpleFOC.

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: Full-bridge stage; STM32G031 (Cortex-M0+); TLE5012B magnetic encoder; OLED
specs.Notes: Cost-reduced/faster successor to S42B (M0+ MCU); Ender/CR-10/Voron/Marlin/Klipper upgrade.
Sensorless homing; most common silent module
External-MOSFET high-current module; up to 20 A coil cap
Enclosed external driver; DIP Iset & µstep; NEMA17/23

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: Dual full-bridge stage; STM32F103C8T6 (M3, 72 MHz); Allegro A1333 12-bit magnetic encoder
specs.Notes: First-gen closed-loop NEMA17; step/dir + serial. Open hardware.

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: 4 half-bridge / 8-MOSFET FOC stage; STM32; 14-bit magnetic encoder
specs.Notes: Quieter, lower-heat FOC upgrade of 42B; max ~1000 RPM. Open hardware.

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: FOC stage; STM32; magnetic encoder; modes CR_vFOC / SR_OPEN / SR_CLOSE / SR_vFOC
specs.Notes: CAN-bus closed-loop NEMA17 — popular for toolhead/CAN builds.

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: FOC vector stage; STM32; high-precision encoder

specs.Notes: Silent stepstick; legacy-mode drop-in for A4988

specs.Package: Internal current sensing (no sense resistors); modest cooling
specs.Notes: Built-in translator + current regulation, internal current sense (ISET resistor, or voltage on MP6500A).

specs.Interface: Step/dir (+ ISET resistor or digital current on MP6500A)
specs.Notes: Translator + current regulation; internal sense; digital current option.
Translator + current regulation, internal sense; OCP/OVP/UVLO/TSD.

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: 2-channel low-saturation H-bridge driver; internal, BTL output
specs.Notes: Old low-voltage 2-phase bipolar stepper driver; small motors / low-V gear. Primary current rating not pinned ⇒ (approx).
Quiet drop-in popular just before the TMC era; runs cool vs A4988.

specs.RMS (A): ~1.5 A (approx; up to ~2.0–2.5 A peak per resellers, approx)
specs.Package: Runs cool relative to A4988; modest heatsink
specs.Notes: PWM constant-current driver, full-step…1/128 µstep, low-vibration. Popular quiet drop-in before TMC.

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: Integrated current sensing (no shunt); Rds-on 0.28 Ω; voltage-mode
specs.Notes: Smart SPI driver w/ internal speed-profile generator (voltage mode KVAL).

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: Integrated 8× N-MOSFET stage, Rds-on 16 mΩ; VDS sensing
specs.Notes: System-in-package: dSPIN controller + integrated 85 V power stage; voltage or current mode.

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: Low-Rds-on stage; external sense; fixed-OFF-time PWM
specs.Notes: µstep to 1/256 with simple step/dir; OC/OT/short protection.

specs.RMS (A): 1.5 A cont. / 2.5 A max per phase
specs.Package: Low Rds-on stage; heatsink for high current
specs.Notes: QFN 4×4 mm, PWM fixed-OFF-time current control, µstep to 1/256. Overcurrent/overtemp/short protection.

specs.RMS (A): Set by external N-channel MOSFETs + sense (scalable; board-dependent)
specs.Package: Determined by chosen external FETs / board layout
specs.Notes: Pre-driver (gate driver only — no internal power stage). SPI-configurable: current/torque DAC, step mode, decay, stall detect. STEP/DIR or PWM. HTSSOP-38.

specs.RMS (A): up to 1.6 A (per output, @24 V/25 °C, with heatsinking)
specs.Package: Lower-current sibling of DRV8825; modest cooling
specs.Notes: Lower-current version of DRV8825 (1.6 A vs 2.5 A) for smaller motors; same STEP/DIR + 1/32 indexer.

specs.RMS (A): up to 2.5 A full-scale (≈1.5 A/phase without heatsink)
specs.Package: Heatsink for >1.5 A/phase; higher V & finer µstep than A4988
specs.Notes: NMOS H-bridges, integrated current sense + indexer, simple STEP/DIR. Pin-similar to A4988 (Vref differs).

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: Integrated current sense (no sense resistor); smart-tune decay
specs.Notes: Like DRV8434S but no SPI (hardware-pin config of µstep/current).

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: Integrated current sense (no sense resistor); smart-tune
specs.Notes: SPI-configurable µstep/current/decay; daisy-chain up to 63. Closest TI analogue to a "smart" driver.

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: Pre-driver only (no internal power stage); external FETs + sense
specs.Notes: Fully custom power stage; SPI sets torque DAC, decay, step mode, stall.
Lower-current DRV8825 (1.6 A vs 2.5 A) for small motors.

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: NMOS H-bridges; integrated current sense + indexer
specs.Notes: Higher V + finer µstep than A4988; pin-similar carrier (Vref differs).
2 A driver w/ auto-tuning current regulation; torque (AT) scaling pins.

specs.RMS (A): TB67S109: 50 V / 4.0 A max rating (≈2.0–2.8 A typical cont.); TB6600 module ~4 A (approx)
specs.Vmot (V): TB67S109: up to 50 V; TB6600 module ~9–42 V (approx)
specs.Package: External heatsink; high-current standalone driver boxes
specs.Notes: TB67S109AFTG: 1-ch bipolar driver, CLK-in (step/dir), 1/32, UVLO/ISD/TSD, WQFN-48. "TB6600" is a common driver-box brand (often a relabelled TB67S109-class part).
The common IC inside many "TB6600" boxes.
AGC (active gain ctrl); higher current than S109

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: ACDS sense-resistor-less current detection; AGC auto gain
specs.Notes: High-current, fine-µstep; AGC boosts current under heavy torque; no shunt needed.
Mid-high current, no sense resistors; popular Pololu carrier.
Tiny 4×4 QFN low-power part (not the 4.5 A S249)

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: Sense resistors on board; silicon varies (often TB67S109-class)
specs.Notes: "TB6600" is a module brand, not one IC — ratings are board-dependent.

specs.Current (A): ~1.2 A RMS (carrier) (approx); IC up to 2.0 A internal
specs.Notes: Pin-configured (CFG0–3, 3-state) silent driver; predecessor of the 2208. No register bus.
Older SPI driver; SG2 sgt, CoolStep, SPI daisy-chain.

specs.Current (A): Set by external FETs; up to ~20 A coil
specs.Rsense/MOSFET: Gate driver for external N-MOSFETs (no internal power stage); external sense
specs.Notes: High-power external-FET driver = TMC5160 without the motion controller.
Silent budget UART driver. No StallGuard/CoolStep. Shared datasheet w/ 2202/2224.

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: Internal MOSFETs; external sense (170 mΩ HS & LS typ)
specs.Notes: Modern default UART driver; sensorless homing + CoolStep.

specs.Current (A): 1.4 A RMS cont.; 1.77 A RMS max (0.11 Ω)
specs.Notes: QFN refresh of TMC2208 (better thermals); driven as tmc2208 in Klipper.

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: Internal MOSFETs; external sense (improved pad vs 2209)
Two 36 V H-bridges, non-dissipative Integrated Current Sensing (ICS) — no external sense; Rref sets full-scale
specs.Notes: Smart mid-current driver w/ on-chip temp/supply ADC + OT/OV prewarning.
Older mid-power SPI driver (Duet/RADDS era). Not silent (no StealthChop).

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: Controller only — pair with TMC6100/6200 + FETs; integrated ADCs & encoder IF
specs.Notes: Hardware FOC servo controller for BLDC/PMSM, 2-phase stepper, DC & voice-coil. All control loops in HW.

specs.Interface: SPI / UART / step-dir (dual-axis); 2× on-chip motion ctrl
specs.Notes: Two bipolar steppers in one chip (dual SixPoint + encoder counter). Niche in 3D printing.

specs.Interface: SPI or UART + step/dir; on-chip motion ctrl
specs.Notes: "cDriver" — integrated driver + SixPoint ramp generator (motion controller). Klipper drives it step/dir as tmc5160-class.

specs.Current (A): FET-limited (~3 A RMS internal-path; higher w/ ext. FETs)
specs.Peak (A): up to 20 A coil w/ external N-MOSFETs
specs.Rsense/MOSFET: Drives external N-MOSFETs (gate driver); external sense
specs.Notes: High-power/high-voltage workhorse; Klipper drives it step/dir.
Integrated-FET TMC5160; same SPI model + Klipper tmc5160 module.

specs.Interface: SPI or UART + step/dir; on-chip motion ctrl
specs.Rsense/MOSFET: Two 36 V H-bridges, Integrated Current Sensing (ICS) — no external sense
specs.Notes: Successor to 5130/2240 class: 8-point ramp generator, DcStep, on-chip ADCs.

specs.Interface: SPI or STEP-equiv. PWM control (gate driver)
specs.Peak (A): drives FETs for up to ~100 A
specs.Rsense/MOSFET: 3-phase MOSFET gate driver (no current sense); prog. gate current 0.5/1/1.5 A
specs.Notes: BLDC/PMSM gate driver, 12–48 V class, 1 W–1 kW. Pairs w/ a FOC controller.

specs.Peak (A): FET-limited (≈ up to 10 A w/ on-chip sense)
specs.Notes: Like 6100 but adds 3× current-sense amps for FOC (no external shunt amps).

specs.Rsense/MOSFET: Integrated 3-phase power stage; charge pump; <50 nA standby
specs.Notes: Low-voltage, battery (1–2 Li-ion) BLDC/PMSM driver; ultra-low standby.

specs.Type: Eddy-current scanner (coil inductance vs distance); contactless. RevH also has nozzle contact detection + built-in accelerometer

specs.Accuracy: Contactless, very fast, can scan a full mesh while moving; temperature-compensated. Beacon Contact (firmware 2.0.0, May 2024) adds nozzle-contact detection for surface-independent Z-offset. RevH adds accelerometer (fw 1.1.0). Connects over USB.

Optional [temperature_probe <name>] (matching name) for drift comp. [stepper_z] endstop_pin: probe:z_virtual_endstop (and position_min: -1 for tap).
items.name: Config sections

items.manufacturer: Coil resonant frequency rises as it nears the metal bed; the chip reports frequency, Klipper maps it to Z. No Z motion needed to read distance.
items.name: Sensing principle

items.manufacturer: LDC_CALIBRATE_DRIVE_CURRENT CHIP=<name> sets the DRIVE_CURRENT0 register — run with the coil ~20 mm above bed center. Sets coil excitation so the resonance sits in range.
items.name: Drive-current calibration

items.manufacturer: PROBE_EDDY_CURRENT_CALIBRATE CHIP=<name> builds the frequency-to-height lookup (manual/"paper" style descent). Cartographer's equivalent is CARTOGRAPHER_CALIBRATE.
items.name: Frequency→Z model

items.manufacturer: default — descend until detected, sample at halt. scan — sample at current Z above each point (mesh; brief pause; SAMPLE_TIME = pause). rapid_scan — sample on the fly, no pause (SAMPLE_TIME = measurement window). tap — descend until nozzle contacts bed, available when tap_threshold set; measurements during lift. e.g. BED_MESH_CALIBRATE METHOD=rapid_scan HORIZONTAL_MOVE_Z=1, PROBE METHOD=tap.
items.name: Probing methods (METHOD=)

items.manufacturer: tap_threshold sets when downward motion halts (force/Δfreq onset). PROBE_EDDY_CURRENT_TAP_CALIBRATE TAP=guess/refine/verify calibrates it. Needs [stepper_z] position_min: -1 and a recommended descend_z: 0.5. Surface-independent ⇒ no temp comp needed for the *offset*.
items.name: Tap (nozzle contact)

items.manufacturer: "Eddy current probes are subject to significant thermal drift." Native path: [temperature_probe] linked by name + TEMPERATURE_PROBE_CALIBRATE PROBE=<name> TARGET=<temp> (uses calibration_position, calibration_extruder_temp, extruder_heating_z, calibration_bed_temp). BTT Eddy USB uses a Generic 3950 NTC on eddy:gpio26; Eddy Coil has none ⇒ no temp comp.
items.name: Temperature drift handling

items.manufacturer: Scan = fast, contactless, but absolute-frequency reading drifts with temp/coil/surface ⇒ needs calibration + temp comp. Touch/Tap = physical contact ⇒ surface-independent, no temp comp, but slower and needs a warm-not-hot nozzle.
items.name: Scan vs Touch trade-off

items.manufacturer: Drop-in Klipper module that adds accurate physical tap Z-offset to LDC1612 probes (notably BTT Eddy and Cartographer). Philosophy: calibrate cold, coarse-home by sensor at any temp, then take a precise tap at print temps — avoiding temperature compensation entirely.

specs.Whatpreinstalled: Klipper ✔ · Moonraker ✔ (approx) · Fluidd ✔ · Mainsail ✗ · KlipperScreen ✔ · webcam via mjpg-streamer + ADXL345 accel libs
specs.Notes: Auto-runs KlipperScreen on a connected PI-TS35/HDMI screen; Fluidd reachable via the board's IP. Unofficial Armbian build framework also exists (redrathnure/armbian-mkspi).

items.name: Any board where a supported base OS runs (Raspberry Pi and other SBCs)

specs.Base distro: N/A — runs on an existing OS you've already flashed
specs.Whatpreinstalled: Installs on demand: Klipper ✔ · Moonraker ✔ · Mainsail ✔ · Fluidd ✔ · KlipperScreen ✔ (plus Crowsnest, Telegram bot, Klipper-Backup, etc.)
specs.Notes: Not a turnkey image — a menu-driven script that installs/updates the Klipper stack onto a plain OS. Needs git. Best results on Debian-based distros (Bullseye era recommended); reported working on Ubuntu 20–22 and Armbian. This is how you put the stack on any base OS in Section 2.

items.name: All Raspberry Pi models (Pi Zero 2 W, Pi 3/4/5, CM4)

specs.Arch: 32-bit armhf and 64-bit arm64; current base = Debian 12 "Bookworm" (older = Debian 11 "Bullseye")
specs.Type: Official Debian-based Pi distro (headless "Lite" variant recommended for Klipper)
specs.Notes: The default, best-tested Klipper host. Use the Lite image (no desktop). KIAUH explicitly recommends Pi OS Lite in either 32- or 64-bit. 64-bit preferred on Pi 3/4/5; 32-bit for very low-RAM/older Pis.

items.name: 300+ ARM boards: Orange Pi, Radxa/Rock, Banana Pi, Odroid, etc. (the usual non-Pi Klipper hosts)

specs.Arch: armhf, aarch64 (arm64), plus x86 via the framework. Image flavors: Minimal (tiny, systemd-networkd), CLI/server (NetworkManager + armbian-config), and Desktop
specs.Type: Community build framework producing Debian and Ubuntu images with patched kernels for ARM SBCs
specs.Notes: The standard way to run Klipper on non-Pi SBCs. Pick a CLI/minimal build and choose the Debian or Ubuntu variant. Install the stack with KIAUH (reported working, not exhaustively tested) or manually.

items.name: Raspberry Pi (all), Odroid, Orange Pi, Pine64/Rock64/ROCKPro64, Radxa Rock 4, native x86 PC/server

specs.Arch: ARMv6, ARMv7, ARMv8 (aarch64), x86_64, and RISC-V (e.g. VisionFive 2, Star64)
specs.Type: Ultra-lightweight Debian-based OS / image (also a script that converts an existing Debian install)
specs.Notes: Minimal footprint, good for low-power hosts. Debian under the hood, so KIAUH/manual Klipper installs work. Broadest architecture coverage of the base options here.

items.name: x86 mini-PCs/servers; certified ARM servers; Raspberry Pi generic 64-bit image (Pi 3/4/5, CM4, Zero 2 W)

specs.Arch: amd64 (x86_64) and arm64 (aarch64) (Ubuntu also supports POWER, s390x, RISC-V — not relevant to Klipper)
specs.Notes: A common choice for x86 hosts and as the Raspberry Pi 64-bit alternative. KIAUH reported working on Ubuntu ~20–22. Use a current LTS.

items.name: x86 PCs/thin-clients/servers and many SBCs (directly or via downstream ports)

specs.Type: Upstream stable Linux (the distro Pi OS/Armbian/DietPi derive from)
specs.Notes: The lowest-common-denominator host. Klipper's only hard need (Linux + Python 3) is met out of the box. Bullseye (11)/Bookworm (12) are the practical targets KIAUH/manual installs aim at.

items.name: Self-contained printers/pads: Creality K1 / K1 Max / KE, Creality Sonic Pad, Sovol Klipper machines

specs.Notes: Klipper is preinstalled by the OEM on a custom/embedded Linux — but typically a locked-down, older Klipper with settings restricted. Source is published for some (e.g. Sonic Pad firmware is open-source, builds under Ubuntu 18.04). Community "rooting"/mod projects (Guilouz Helper Script, K1 klipper-mod, Guppy) unlock Moonraker/Mainsail/Fluidd and a current Klipper. Not a general-purpose host OS.

specs.Properties: Easiest to print, rigid but brittle, low heat resistance (Tg ~60 °C), biodegradable. Best for visual/prototype parts.
PLA blend with improved toughness/impact vs standard PLA; same easy printing. E.g. Polymaker PolyMax PLA 190–230 °C.
PLA with additives for glossy "silk" sheen; slightly higher temps for shine, weaker layer adhesion, not for functional parts.
Tough, good layer adhesion, chemical/moisture resistant, mild flex. Stringing-prone; glue/release on bed. Good all-rounder.
Strong, impact- and heat-resistant, acetone-smoothable. Warps badly; emits fumes (ventilate).
ABS-like with superior UV/weather resistance; best for outdoor parts. Warps; fumes; needs heat.
Flexible/elastic rubber-like; abrasion resistant. Print slow (20–30 mm/s), short retraction; direct-drive preferred.
Much softer/stretchier than 95A. Very challenging to feed: requires direct-drive, constrained path, ~15–20 mm/s, near-zero retraction.
Strong, tough, abrasion/fatigue resistant, low friction. Highly hygroscopic — must be dried before printing; warps. PA12 lower-moisture than PA6.
Carbon/glass-fiber-filled nylon: high stiffness/strength, dimensional stability. Abrasive — hardened nozzle required; dry well; anneal for best props (e.g. 80–100 °C / 6–16 h).
Very high strength + heat resistance (~110–130 °C), impact resistant, can be optically clear. Warps strongly; hygroscopic; needs all-metal hotend.
Carbon-fiber PC: stiffer, more dimensionally stable, less warp than neat PC. Abrasive — hardened nozzle; all-metal hotend + enclosure.
PC/ABS alloy: balances PC's heat/strength with ABS's easier printing and toughness. Enclosure recommended; fumes.
High-impact polystyrene. Lightweight; dissolvable in limonene → common breakaway/soluble support for ABS. Warps like ABS.
Water-soluble support material (pairs with PLA/PETG). Extremely hygroscopic — keep dry; clogs if degraded by overheating.
Water-soluble support (butenediol vinyl alcohol); dissolves faster/more reliably than PVA, pairs with more materials. Hygroscopic.
Prints like PLA; smooths with isopropyl alcohol (IPA) for glossy/transparent finish. Hygroscopic — store dry.
Lightweight (floats), fatigue/chemical resistant, living hinges. Very poor bed adhesion (needs PP tape/packaging-tape); warps.
High-performance: extreme heat/chemical/wear resistance, high strength. Needs high-temp hotend + heated chamber (~90–160 °C) + high-temp thermistor.
PEEK-family high-performer; slower crystallization makes it somewhat easier to print than PEEK. Heated enclosure ~70–150 °C.
Aerospace-grade: flame/smoke/heat resistant, high strength-to-weight. Requires high-temp hotend, heated bed + chamber. (Prusa lists PEI nozzle ~430 °C / bed 150 °C.)
Carbon-fiber PLA: stiffer, matte finish, better dimensional stability than PLA; still low heat resistance. Abrasive — hardened nozzle.
Carbon-fiber PETG: increased stiffness/heat-deflection and matte look vs PETG; tougher than PLA-CF. Abrasive — hardened nozzle.
Glass-fiber fill (in PA, PP, PETG, etc.): boosts stiffness, strength, and heat-deflection; less stiff but tougher than equivalent CF. Abrasive — hardened nozzle.
PLA (usually) + wood particles for wood-like look/finish, sandable/stainable. Prints near base PLA but clog-prone — use ≥0.5 mm nozzle; particles mildly abrasive.
PLA (usually) + metal powder (bronze/copper/steel): heavy, metallic look, polishable. Highly abrasive — hardened/wear-resistant nozzle required; clog-prone.
Easiest, rigid, brittle, low heat-resist; visual/proto parts.
Toughness/impact-boosted PLA blend; same easy printing.
Impact-modified for functional/jig parts; ABS-like toughness, PLA ease.
Reformulated for ≥24 mm³/s flow; ~100–300 mm/s; keeps ~90% props at speed.
Anneal/crystallize for heat resistance far above normal PLA.
Mineral-filled for flat/matte finish, hides layer lines; slightly brittle.
Glitter/mica flakes for sparkle; flakes mildly abrasive, can clog small nozzles.
Stone-powder fill for marble look, slight texture; mildly abrasive.
PLA + wood particles; sandable/stainable; clog-prone — use ≥0.5 mm nozzle.
PLA + metal powder (bronze/copper/steel); heavy, polishable.
Phosphorescent (strontium aluminate) fill; very abrasive; charge under light.
Carbon-black fill; ~30 Ω·cm vol. resistivity (XY print); semi-flexible, weak Z adhesion.
Carbon-fiber PLA: stiffer, matte, dim. stable; still low heat-resist.
Stiffer/clearer than PETG, harder to print (narrow window); hygroscopic.
Tougher, more impact-resistant & clearer than PETG; stiffer; less stringy.
Carbon-fiber PETG: stiffer, higher HDT, matte; tougher than PLA-CF.
Toughened, lower-warp ABS blend; easier than plain ABS.
Carbon-fiber ABS: stiffer, dim. stable, matte, less warp.
Carbon-fiber ASA: UV-stable + stiff/dim. stable; outdoor functional.
Even softer than 85A; very challenging to feed; direct-drive only.
Broad rubber-elastomer class (incl. PEBA); softer/grippier, very hygroscopic; slow.
Strong, tough, abrasion/fatigue resist; very hygroscopic (~3% water); warps.
Lower moisture (~0.5%), stabler in wet conditions vs PA6; less stiff.
PA6/PA12 copolymer: balance of strength + low-moisture between the two.
CF-filled nylon: high stiffness/strength, dim. stable; dry well; anneal for best props.
Glass-fiber nylon: stiff, strong, high HDT; tougher but less stiff than PA-CF.
Very high strength + heat + impact, can be clear; warps; hygroscopic.
PC/PBT alloy: high impact + chemical resist, good low-temp toughness.
Lightweight (floats), fatigue/chem resist, living hinges; very poor bed adhesion (use PP tape); warps.
CF PP: stiffer, higher HDT, very light; chem resist; still adhesion-tricky.
Semi-crystalline: chem/flame/heat resist, good electricals; CF grade common; brittle-ish.
High-temp nylon (polyphthalamide); CF grade ~48% stronger/102% stiffer than PA6-CF.
Amorphous: better impact/chem resist than PEI, steam-sterilizable; extreme temps.
Pure PEI: max heat resist, flame/smoke rated, high strength-to-weight.
PEI+PC blend: aerospace-certified, more ductile/impact-resistant, easier than 1010.
Non-soluble; snaps off cleanly, minimal post-processing; pairs w/ PLA/Tough PLA/ABS/Nylon/PETG.
